---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


Eye Tracking Scanpath Visualization
Data files Used: test1_F_S.csv (fixations & saccades)
      test1_gazetracking_clean.csv (raw gaze path)
David_Carson.jpg (background stimulus image)


```{r}
#install.packages(c("ggplot2", "jpeg", "grid", "dplyr", "scales", "ggrepel"))

#library(ggplot2)
#library(jpeg)
#library(grid)
#library(dplyr)
#library(scales)
#library(ggrepel)

# Load data 
fs   <- read.csv("test1_F_S.csv")
gaze <- read.csv("test1_gazetracking_clean.csv")

# Extract fixations (rows that have Fixation X/Y)
fixations <- fs %>%
  filter(!is.na(Fixation.X) & !is.na(Fixation.Y)) %>%
  select(
    fixation_index = Fixation.Index.by.Stimulus,
    x              = Fixation.X,
    y              = Fixation.Y,
    duration       = Fixation.Duration
  ) %>%
  arrange(fixation_index)

# Cleaning gaze data column names
colnames(gaze) <- c("timestamp", "gaze_x", "gaze_y")

# Loading background image
list.files(pattern = "jpg|jpeg|JPG")
bg_img <- readJPEG("David Carson.jpg")
img_w    <- ncol(bg_img)   # 1920
img_h    <- nrow(bg_img)   # 1200

# Fliping Y axis 
# Eye-tracker origin is top-left; ggplot origin is bottom-left
fixations <- fixations %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - y)

gaze_plot <- gaze %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - gaze_y)

# Plot 1: Scanpath (fixations + saccades) 

scanpath_plot <- ggplot() +

  # Background image
  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # Saccade lines connecting fixations in order
  geom_path(data    = fixations,
            aes(x   = x, y = y_flip),
            colour  = "white",
            linewidth = 0.7,
            alpha   = 0.8,
            lineend = "round") +

  # Fixation circles — size proportional to duration
  geom_point(data   = fixations,
             aes(x  = x, y = y_flip, size = duration),
             shape  = 21,
             fill   = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.45),
             colour = "white",
             stroke = 1.2) +

  # Fixation order labels
  geom_label(data        = fixations,
             aes(x       = x, y = y_flip,
                 label   = fixation_index),
             size        = 3,
             fontface    = "bold",
             colour      = "white",
             fill        = scales::alpha("black", 0.55),
             label.size  = 0,
             label.padding = unit(0.15, "lines")) +

  # Scales & theme
  scale_size_continuous(name   = "Fixation Duration (ms)",
                        range  = c(4, 18)) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Scanpath",
       subtitle = "Circles = fixation duration | Lines = saccades | Numbers = fixation order",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("scanpath_plot.png",
       plot   = scanpath_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,   
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: scanpath_plot.png\n")

# Plot 2: Raw Gaze Path

gaze_path_plot <- ggplot() +

  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # Continuous gaze trace coloured by time
  geom_path(data = gaze_plot,
            aes(x = gaze_x, y = y_flip, colour = timestamp),
            linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.7, lineend = "round") +

  scale_colour_viridis_c(name   = "Time (ms)",
                         option = "plasma") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Raw Gaze Path",
       subtitle = "Colour = time progression (early → late)",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("gaze_path_plot.png",
       plot   = gaze_path_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: gaze_path_plot.png\n")

# Plot 3: Heatmap of Gaze Density

heatmap_plot <- ggplot() +

  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  stat_density_2d(data = gaze_plot,
                  aes(x = gaze_x, y = y_flip, fill = after_stat(density)),
                  geom  = "raster",
                  contour = FALSE,
                  alpha = 0.55,
                  h     = c(80, 80)) +

  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("transparent", "#0000FF55", "#00FF0099", "#FFFF00CC", "#FF0000"),
    name    = "Gaze Density") +

  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title    = "Eye Tracking — Gaze Density Heatmap",
       subtitle = "Warmer colours = more time spent looking",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_void(base_size = 13) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(colour = "white", face = "bold",
                                   hjust = 0.5, size = 16,
                                   margin = margin(b = 4)),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(colour = "grey80", hjust = 0.5, size = 10),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text     = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.title    = element_text(colour = "white"),
    legend.key      = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)
  )

ggsave("heatmap_plot.png",
       plot   = heatmap_plot,
       width  = img_w / 100,
       height = img_h / 100 + 1.2,
       dpi    = 100,
       bg     = "black")

cat("✔  Saved: heatmap_plot.png\n")
cat("\nAll three plots saved! Open them in your Files pane.\n")
```
Animation:

Eye Tracking — "Through Their Eyes" Animation
Replays raw gaze data as a moving dot over the stimulus/poster
```{r}
#install.packages(c("gganimate", "gifski"))


#library(ggplot2)
#library(jpeg)
#library(grid)
#library(dplyr)
#library(gganimate)
#library(gifski)   # used for creating GIF frames

# Loading data

gaze <- read.csv("test1_gazetracking_clean.csv")
colnames(gaze) <- c("timestamp", "gaze_x", "gaze_y")

# Adding a frame index (each row = one frame of animation)
gaze <- gaze %>%
  mutate(frame = row_number())

# Loading the background image 

bg_img <- readJPEG("David Carson.jpg")
img_w  <- ncol(bg_img)   # 1920
img_h  <- nrow(bg_img)   # 1200

#Flip Y (eye tracker: top-left origin → ggplot: bottom-left) 

gaze <- gaze %>%
  mutate(y_flip = img_h - gaze_y)

# Building a "trail"
# For each frame, we keep the last N gaze points so the dot
# leaves a fading tail — feels more like a real eye movement

trail_length <- 20   # how many past points to show as the trail

gaze_with_trail <- lapply(gaze$frame, function(f) {
  start <- max(1, f - trail_length + 1)
  slice <- gaze[start:f, ]
  slice$current_frame <- f
  slice$age <- seq_len(nrow(slice))   # 1 = oldest in trail, N = current
  slice
}) %>%
  bind_rows()

# Opacity and size fade: current point is big & opaque, trail fades out
gaze_with_trail <- gaze_with_trail %>%
  mutate(
    alpha_val = age / trail_length,           # 0 = transparent, 1 = opaque
    size_val  = ifelse(age == trail_length,   # current point is larger
                       6, 2)
  )

# 5. Building the animation

p <- ggplot() +

  # Background stimulus image (static)
  annotation_raster(bg_img,
                    xmin = 0, xmax = img_w,
                    ymin = 0, ymax = img_h) +

  # Fading gaze trail
  geom_point(data  = gaze_with_trail,
             aes(x     = gaze_x,
                 y     = y_flip,
                 alpha = alpha_val,
                 size  = size_val),
             colour = "#00C8FF",
             shape  = 16) +

  # Current gaze point is shown as bright white ring on top
  geom_point(data   = gaze_with_trail %>% filter(age == trail_length),
             aes(x  = gaze_x, y = y_flip),
             colour = "white",
             fill   = scales::alpha("#00C8FF", 0.6),
             shape  = 21,
             size   = 10,
             stroke = 2) +

  scale_alpha_identity() +
  scale_size_identity() +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, img_w), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, img_h), expand = c(0, 0)) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_void() +
  theme(plot.background = element_rect(fill = "black", colour = NA)) +

  # gganimate --> step through each frame
  transition_manual(current_frame) +
  labs(title = "Gaze replay  |  t = {current_frame * 8} ms")

#Render and save
# nframes = number of gaze samples (2042)
# fps     = controls playback speed. 
#           Real time ≈ 120 fps (data is ~8ms/sample).
#           Use 60 for half-speed, 30 for slow-motion.

cat("Rendering animation — this takes 1-2 minutes...\n")

anim <- animate(
  p,
  nframes  = nrow(gaze),
  fps      = 60,            # change to 30 for slower speed, 120 for real-time speed
  width    = 960,           # half of 1920 to keep file size reasonable
  height   = 600,           # half of 1200
  renderer = gifski_renderer("gaze_animation.gif")
)

cat("✔  Saved: gaze_animation.gif\n")
cat("   Open it in your browser or Files pane to watch!\n")

# Saving as MP4 --->  (needs ffmpeg installed)
# anim_save("gaze_animation.mp4",
#           animation = animate(p, nframes = nrow(gaze), fps = 60,
#                               width = 960, height = 600,
#                               renderer = ffmpeg_renderer()))
```
